D9 BiologyL5 EvaluateReference

Octopus Iridophore + Leucophore Layers

octopus skin stacks three cell types — chromatophores (pigment), iridophores (tunable Bragg reflectors), leucophores (white scatterers) — giving structural blue/green plus broadband background

Mäthger 2009: iridophores in octopus skin are stacks of platelet proteins forming a 1D photonic Bragg reflector — narrow band reflection, tunable by changing layer spacing. Above the iridophores sit chromatophores (selective pigment). Below sit leucophores (broadband white scatterers). Layered control gives the octopus its uncanny matching.

Toggle layer visibility to see what each contributes.
